steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says archaeological evidence of "significant increases in clam size and abundance" concurrent with the Kwakwaka'wakw terraced garden practice "supports the conclusion that ______." The correct conclusion must be directly supported by that specific evidence — larger, more abundant clams — and nothing beyond it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the practice...not only effectively maintained a food source but also promoted its robustness: increased size and abundance directly show the clam population was maintained and grew stronger. This is exactly what the evidence supports. ✓
  • Choice B — non-Indigenous people...adopted the practice after observing its efficacy: the passage says nothing about non-Indigenous adoption of the practice. This goes beyond the evidence. ✗
  • Choice C — there is greater corroboration...of Kwakwaka'wakw songs than of Temiar and Lakota songs: the passage never compares the archaeological support for different groups' songs. Only the Kwakwaka'wakw evidence is discussed. ✗
  • Choice D — contemporary Kwakwaka'wakw people no longer implement those methods: the passage gives no information about whether the practice is still used today. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
